latest.title: Closed Period Notification
latest.content:
1 May 2026 14:50
RNS Number : 8652C
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
01 May 2026
LONDON STOCK EXCHANGE
ANNOUNCEMENT
JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC
(the 'Company') Closed Period Notification
Legal Entity Identifier:
549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
Information disclosed in accordance with UK Market Abuse Regulation ('MAR') Article 19, paragraph 11
The Company announces that it is satisfied that any inside information which the Directors and the Company may have in the period leading up to the announcement of its results for the half year ended 30th April 2026 has previously been, and will continue to be, notified to the London Stock Exchange ('LSE') via a regulatory information service. Therefore, the Company is not prohibited from dealing in its own securities.
The mandatory closed period under MAR will commence on 11th May 2026 and will expire when the results in respect of the half year ended 30th April 2026 are published, which is expected to be on or around 22nd June 2026.
If in the period leading up to the announcement of the half year results the Directors of the Company come into the possession of any inside information, this will be notified to a RIS (the London Stock Exchange) without delay and before any such transactions are
undertaken.
JPMorgan Funds Limited - Company Secretary
1st May 2026
For further information, please
contact:
Joel Clopon
JPMorgan Funds Limited 0800 20 40 20

latest.title: Net Asset Value(s)
latest.content:
1 May 2026 12:10
RNS Number : 8424C
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
01 May 2026
JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC (the 'Company')
Legal Entity Identifier: 549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
THE COMPANY ANNOUNCES THE UNAUDITED NET ASSET VALUE (NAV) As at: 30 April 2026
THE NET ASSET VALUE PER SHARE IN PENCE, INCLUDING INCOME WITH DEBT AT FAIR VALUE:
66.69
The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.
Name of contact and telephone number for queries:
Paul Ainger 0044 207 742 6524

Date: 01 May 2026

company_30s.what_they_do: JEMA Ltd is a London‑listed investment vehicle that holds stakes in a handful of companies, mainly in infrastructure and energy.  It manages a portfolio of assets through its S account, which distributes dividends and calculates a net asset value for shareholders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Because JEMA sits at the crossroads of UK and Russian markets, its holdings give it influence over key industrial assets that could benefit from geopolitical shifts.  Its NAV of £7.18 per share signals that the market is trading far below the underlying value of its portfolio.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been under intense scrutiny from sanctions and court proceedings, which has pushed its share price to a very low level.  Operations continue, but cash flow and growth prospects are unclear.  The stock trades at a fraction of its NAV, suggesting a deep discount.

rns_narrative: JEMA has issued a series of routine updates: a closed‑period notice, a net‑asset‑value report, a list of its ten largest investments, and a disclosure of its holdings.  A recent Russian court case update and an AGM result are the most material items.

These events can be grouped into three themes: 1) Legal and sanctions exposure – the court case and potential de‑sanctioning; 2) Transparency – regular NAV and holdings disclosures; 3) Governance – AGM outcomes that could signal strategic shifts.

The company is keeping the market informed while awaiting the outcome of the court case and any de‑sanctioning.  If a favorable ruling arrives or a new investment is announced, the stock could begin to recover.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: The community is largely silent, waiting for a catalyst.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter is minimal, centered on the possibility that sanctions will be lifted and the company’s NAV will be reflected in the price.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to ongoing sanctions, legal uncertainty, and the lack of a clear growth plan.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat; a single positive announcement could trigger a sharp mood swing.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, which is a good sign for a contrarian play – the market is not primed for a surprise.

near_term_catalysts: A favourable decision in the Russian court case that could remove sanctions.
An AGM resolution to pursue a new investment or partnership that would inject capital.
A fresh funding round or capital injection from institutional investors.
Positive news from the Budapest negotiations that could signal a policy shift.
A significant volume spike that indicates institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months: price breaking above the 200‑day moving average, volume doubling, a new investment announcement, or a positive court ruling.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is in the early stage of a deep crash, trading at a very low level.  The market’s compression score suggests a moderately tight environment, so a breakout would likely signal mean‑reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is trading far below its NAV, offering a huge upside if it recovers even modestly.  A 50% rebound would more than double the price, and a 100% rebound would double that again.  Historical deep‑crash signals have produced large returns, so a bounce could be explosive.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that sanctions remain in place and the court case turns negative, leaving the company stuck at a very low price.  The worst‑case scenario is a continued flat or further decline, with no recovery.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: The risk is effectively limited to the current price staying flat, while the upside could be several times that price if the share climbs toward its NAV.  The asymmetry is highly favourable for a contrarian bet.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed breakout above the 200‑day moving average and volume at least twice the average.  Size the position at 5–10% of your portfolio and set a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a short position until you see a positive court ruling or a new investment announcement.  Trigger entry if the price moves above 0.02p with a volume spike.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks at this time.

ai_insights.bottom_line: JEMA’s share is deeply discounted; a pending court decision or sanctions lift could spark a mean‑reversion bounce, delivering a highly asymmetric upside if the price climbs even modestly.
